finding: Lowering subsidy level for antimalarials from 92% to 80% improves targeting (share going to malaria-positive patients increases from 56% to 75%) without reducing access among children, because adult demand is price-elastic while children's demand is inelastic.

Single women reduce stated career ambitions, desired salary, and willingness to travel when peers can observe their answers, but only when male peers are present; effect is driven by marriage-market signaling rather than general humility or observability per se.

Emigration to democracies reduces support for non-democratic parties at home, with a 1 percentage point increase in westward migration reducing communist votes by 0.6 percentage points; effect operates through information transmission and is stronger when destination countries have higher democratic standards.

Negative calorie illusion (underestimating calories when healthy foods are present) occurs only when a reference food anchors estimates; without reference point, presence of healthy side dish has no effect, suggesting anchoring-and-adjustment mechanism rather than averaging of healthiness judgments.

Population-level pragmatic reasoning appears Gricean, but individual-level data show heterogeneity: most speakers are level-1 Gricean reasoners but most listeners are exhaustive level-1 reasoners rather than level-2 Gricean listeners, with Bayesian model comparison favoring the heterogeneous type model.

Triple scarcity effect (simultaneous absence of liquidity, loss-frame, and lending alternatives) drives recent payday borrowers to request $725 (45% above need); dimensions act synergistically rather than additively, with recent borrowers showing stronger effects than new borrowers on all three scarcity dimensions.
